title: phlegmariurus urbanii
intro: Phlegmariurus urbanii, also known as the Urban's climbing forked polypody, is a species of climbing fern native to South America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and supporting the immune system. Herbal preparations of Phlegmariurus urbanii are often made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are used to treat a range of ailments, although further research is needed to confirm its effectiveness.
